routed_checks.description: Every empirical sub-question, each as 'empirical question -> named cheapest decisive check' (e.g. 'MRR claim -> request payment-processor export'). Checks outrank debates on questions a check can settle.

tensions.description: Evidence-vs-evidence tensions in the record. NOT bull/bear stances: each side cites its own evidence. A tension whose sides cannot each anchor to a signal is not a tension.

name.description: One line naming the tension, e.g. 'deck install-count vs registry downloads'.

evidence_refs.description: Signal ids backing this side. At least one — a side with no evidence is not a side.
Signal ids backing this side. At least one.

crux.description: ALWAYS non-blank. The sharpest judgment fork no routed check can settle — posture, risk-tolerance, conviction. For a clean record with no live fork, the dominant what-would-have-to-be-true. This seeds the room.

room_brief.description: What the room must cover — contradictions, gaps, cold-start, the wedge. Guidance for seating, not a verdict.
